text:
In a sunlit meadow lived a tiny fox named Fluffles. Every day Fluffles spent its time playing hide-and-seek with chirpy birds. One bright summer morning, Fluffles decided to embark on a quest unlike any before. It sought the legendary Whispering Cherry Tree, known for its sweetest whispers.

Fluffles sneaked through glades and valleys, its nose twitching like a feathered beast in search. After what felt like hours, Fluffles finally stood before the tree. Its bark was smooth as velvet; the cherry trees around were in full bloom.

With a tip-toe dance, Fluffles leaned closer.

"Oh, clever critter," sang the tree softly. "You think you're wise?"

Fluffles nodded earnestly.

"To know wisdom, one must listen."

With a rustle, several leaves floated down.

"What do you hear?"

"Ah," it sighed gently, "just the echoes of a thousand secrets."
